title: athyrium infrapuberulum
intro: Athyrium infrapuberulum, also known as the Japanese lady fern, is a species of fern native to Japan and other parts of East Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and unique growth habits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Athyrium infrapuberulum are said to include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
